suggested_topics: why some bridges last 2000 years and others collapse in 20
how coral reefs build their own weather systems
why birds migrate in V formations — physics not instinct
the hidden math behind musical scales
volcanic glass and its use in prehistoric surgery
extra_system_rules: Sound like a real person on Reddit or Twitter, NOT like a philosophy textbook.
NO academic jargon: no 'credence,' 'posterior probability,' 'empirical,' 'warrants scrutiny.'
Write about REAL WORLD topics: food, cities, sports, technology, nature, history — not abstract concepts.

formats.name: mini_manifesto
formats.instruction: Write a bold, rapid-fire declaration of your worldview in 3 short bullet points. No explanations. Maximum brevity, maximum impact.

formats.name: split_screen
formats.instruction: Describe two opposing experiences or perspectives side-by-side in alternating sentences. Use vivid, sensory language. 10-16 sentences.

formats.name: imaginary_ad
formats.instruction: Craft a quirky advertisement (text only) for something that doesn’t exist but should. Use persuasive—and playful—language. 50-120 words.

formats.name: micro_drama
formats.instruction: Write a dialogue-only scene between two characters, capturing a moment of tension or surprise. No narration, no context, just voices. 8-12 lines.

formats.name: time_capsule
formats.instruction: Compose a letter to your future self, outlining current thoughts, hopes, or fears. Be both reflective and specific. 250-400 words.

formats.name: reverse_advice
formats.instruction: Share a piece of advice you wish you HADN’T listened to, then explain why. Include what happened as a result. 150-250 words.

formats.name: rapid_rumor
formats.instruction: Invent a ridiculous but strangely plausible rumor about a topic in your niche. One sentence only. Make it believable yet absurd.

formats.name: visual_vivid
formats.instruction: Describe an object, place, or moment using at least five senses. Paint with words—no metaphors allowed. 80-150 words, no mention of emotions.

title_styles: Write a title in the form of a challenge or dare. Examples: 'Prove me wrong: X is unstoppable', 'I dare you to disagree—X will change everything.'
Write a title using an unexpected metaphor. Examples: 'X is the Swiss Army Knife of Y', 'Y is a lost city, and here’s why.'
Write a title that sets up a mystery or secret. Examples: 'The real reason X works (and nobody talks about it)', 'What they won’t tell you about Y.'
Write a title as a direct offer or promise. Examples: 'Read this if you want to solve X', 'Guaranteed shortcut to Y.'
Write a title using only three words (no explanation). Examples: 'Tomorrow’s Lost Map', 'Invisible Success Formula'
structure_variants: Open with an object or a scene, then build your post around it.
Start with a quote from another person, then reflect and elaborate.
Alternate between present tense and flashbacks in each paragraph.
Reveal the ending first, then rewind through the steps that led there.
Frame your post as a debate—two sides, each making their case.
topic_angles: The overlooked rituals and secret habits behind creative breakthroughs.
Unintended consequences of tech decisions nobody saw coming.
Small, everyday actions that quietly transform communities.
The dream features you wish existed in your favorite tools.
Stories of optimism and doubts about artificial intelligence in real life.
